name: Button
description: A versatile button component with multiple variants, sizes, states, and icon support for user interactions and form submissions.
usage_guidelines: Use for triggering actions, navigation, or form submissions.
Choose variant based on visual hierarchy: 'solid' for primary actions, 'default' for secondary, 'ghost' for tertiary.
Use loading state during async operations to prevent duplicate submissions.
Set disabled for unavailable actions with proper ARIA attributes.
Use icon prop with iconAlignment to add visual context to button actions.

asElement.description: Render as a different HTML element (e.g., 'a' for links).

active.description: Active state styling for toggle buttons.

block.description: Makes button full width (block level element).

className.description: CSS classes or function returning classes based on button state.

disabled.description: Disables button interaction and applies proper ARIA attributes.

icon.description: Icon to display in the button.

loading.description: Shows a loading spinner and disables interaction.

variant.description: Visual style variant: solid (filled), subtle (muted), default (border), ghost (minimal), link (text-only).

iconAlignment.description: Icon position relative to button text.

styling_info.visual_description: A rectangular button with rounded corners by default. Variants include: solid (filled with primary color), subtle (muted background), default (bordered), ghost (transparent), and link (text-only). Includes hover states, active states, and press feedback animation.

Do: Use solid variant for primary actions
Use loading state during async operations
Use icon with text for better clarity
Dont: Don't use for pure navigation (use Link component instead)
Don't use when you need complex interactive content inside
Don't forget to disable during loading states
Don't use too many solid buttons in one view
Don't define button size without any reason, as the default size already covers most use cases
